pages.raw_text:
With a we'll filled courtroom OF
spectators on hand, but the intense
interest Of the former trials no.
ticeably absent, the trial of the six
remaining I. w. w. "conspirators'
commenced this morning before
Justice George w. Stocker.


With Fred III. Moore and Thomas
j. Symmes of Chicago represent.
ing the prisoner, and Deputy Prose.
cutor Don F. Kizer and Deputy Cor
poration Counsel John f. Blair
prosecuting the cases, seceral lively
tilts were INDUCED in, although the
enmity of counsel that marked the
first trials before Judge Stocker is
lacking.


The morning was spent in intro.
ducing l. w. w. literature as evi.
dence and the reading of articles
posters, etc, to the jury. Detective
Burns was placed on the stand, but
had given little testimony before
the noon recess.


The six l. w. w. leaders charged
with conspiracy are George Speed
who came here from San Francis.
CO; John Nj. Foss, Otto Justh, Hart
well Shippey, Charles Grant and
Thomas Whitehead.


The six jurors who are hearing
the evidence in the case are J. H.
Tilsley, w. H. Wiscomb. A G. An
sell, NJ. G. Duncan, NJ. NJ. Dickson
and Z. A. Winchester. Thomas J.
Symmes of Chicago was sent here
to take the place of David K Tone
who was due here today to assist
Attorney Moore in the fight.
